name: Guidelines for Cyber Security Roles
description: Offers strategic guidance on roles and responsibilities at a technical and soft skill level for this within the information technology, business acumen, leadership, communication and relationship space.

name: Guidelines for Cyber Security Incidents
description: Organised strategic approach to detecting and managing cyber attacks in a way that minimizes damage including reputational, recovery time and total costs

name: Guidelines for Procurement and Outsourcing
description: Risk management approach to the procurement and acceptance of IT equipment, services and solutions including supply chain considerations and timing

name: Guidelines for Security Documentation
description: Specification of documented artifacts to drive, support and monitor security strategies and execution within an organisation

name: Guidelines for Physical Security
description: Application of defence-in-depth principles for the protection of systems enhanced through the use of physical controls

name: Guidelines for Personnel Security
description: Outlines continous educational requirements for all government personnel including contractors and volunteers to assist with understanding of their security responsibilities

name: Guidelines for Communications Infrastructure
description: Recommended approach for the installation and maintenance of required services within facilities located on Australian soil

name: Guidelines for Communications Systems
description: Identifies techniques and procedures for the maintenance and management of communication infrastructure and services supporting operational outcomes

name: Guidelines for Enterprise Mobility
description: Guidance on implementation, maintenace and use of mobile devices for official government purposes on Australian and Foriegn soil

name: Guidelines for Evaluated Products
description: Sets forth recommendations for the selection and implementation of Evaluated Products identified through Common Criteria or the Evaluated Product Listing

name: Guidelines for ICT Equipment
description: Recommended practical approach to lifecycle management of the physcial ICT Equipment

name: Guidelines for Media
description: Focused guidance on the management and maintenance of volatile and non volatile media

name: Guidelines for System Hardening
description: Recommended approach to tools technqiues and prefernital practices to reducing vulnerability in technology, applications, systems, infrastructure, firmware and other areas.

name: Guidelines for System Management
description: Guided administration management of information management systems in the enterprise network to facilitate secure delivery of IT Services

name: Guidelines for System Monitoring
description: Provides practical guidance on keeping track of performance, availability, monitoring and other key metrics of an information technology system

name: Guidelines for Software Development
description: Provides key frameworks for developing software in a structured, systematic and efficient manner

name: Guidelines for Database Systems
description: Outlines key factors for the desiging, implementing, and managing of databases in a structured manner

name: Guidelines for Email
description: Framework for using email in a official capacity including covering aspects such as email etiquette, email security, email retention, and email management.

name: Guidelines for Networking
description: Recommended approach for the implementation for network topology, network protocols, network security, network performance, and network management

name: Guidelines for Cryptography
description: Considerations when applying cryptographic techniques covering encryption algorithms, key management, cryptographic protocols, and cryptographic implementations.

name: Guidelines for Gateways
description: Key objectives when designing, implementing, and managing gateways that facilitate communication between different networks or devices.

name: Guidelines for Data Transfers
description: Applied techniques and considerations for transferring data between different systems or devices in a secure, efficient, and compliant manner.
